identity.canonical_title: Compact Worked Example (Explicit Composite Streamfunction): ζ

theory.canonical_statement: You can compute: - velocity field \((u,v)\), - instantaneous streamlines (contours of \(\Psi\)), - vorticity \(\zeta=\nabla^2_{\!s}\Psi\), - potential vorticity \(q=\zeta+f\), and track how \(\Psi_{\rm wave}\) shifts energy between hemispheric arms through \(J(\Psi,\zeta+f)\).
theory.technical_interpretation: Compact Worked Example (Explicit Composite Streamfunction): ζ is a differential equation, written ζ=∇^2_ sΨ. It relates the quantity to its derivatives, governing how the system evolves in space and time. It is built from ∇, Ψ, s, defining ζ. Within the Trawin Zero Point registry it serves as one element of the framework's mathematical narrative.

formation.note: Introduced in the derivation where , - potential vorticity , and track how shifts energy between hemispheric arms through .
